reviews.review_description:
Update: Can't really recommend it because sadly it was almost dead on arrival and hasn't got any better. I mostly played vs bots and that got tired quick.

Old:
Love what I've seen so far! It plays really smooth. The unlocking and levelling up is really addictive. Matches are short and not overwhelming. Graphics and sound are very nice.
I hope the active player base will grow some more and hero balance will be actively monitored.
Only wished I waited 2 days with my purchase because it's heavily discounted already...

Loved this starting with the beta version
 Had to upgrade to Q3 when the full version was released just to play. Not happy to lose rank points when opponents disconnect tho. Why do youlose more points when losing than you accumulate by winning? Seems backwards. Anyhow, I'm addicted.

This is one of the toughest reviews to write because it’s honestly such a fun and brilliant game, once you pick it up the gameplay is just a ton of fun and addicting. Maybe one of my all time favorites. 

However while I want to show support, there’s a huge flaw here. The devs made no real single player content whatsoever. No story mode, no campaign, not even any unique battle modes besides just a basic ai scrimmage which gets old quickly. 

This would all be excusable if there was a thriving online/multiplayer community, however that’s yet to materialize and It’s been really rare to find a real match in game. So the devs built a purely multiplayer game with no multiplayer community to support it. Hopefully more people join or more single player content is on the way because if not, this promising game is dead on arrival.

(Will gladly change this review if either of those issues change)

developer_response.body:
Hi Dico!

Thank you so much for posting your review. This is all great feedback, and I'll be passing it along to the team.

Regarding AI matches, we are aware that while the game grows, there are often times during the day where player populations are a bit low. To try to solve for this, we've established community play events in our Discord, which is quite active. There you can find fellow Breakers coordinating events, discussing comps, and planning tournaments. We encourage you to join one of these play events and see how you feel!

Thank you again for taking time to write a review. We really appreciate it. :)

reviews.review_title: Great game but would prefer campaign
reviews.review_description: I love everything about this game except it’s multiplayer focus. It reminds me of Brass Tactics in some ways, which was one of my all-time favorite Rift games. I would love to see this game release additional single-player campaign content. Otherwise, 3 stars, with an extra star for Horizon+ availability.
It’s more like a strategy game than previous games in the Moss series. I like the game, and it seems to have an active multiplayer community.

reviews.review_title: Awesome game, it’s an absolute blast 🤩
reviews.review_description: Glassbreakers is a blast to play, short rounds, ten minutes each. It’s such a satisfying win. I loved Moss 1 and 2. I thought this one was a slower turn taking table top game but was pleasantly surprised that its nothing like that and is action and strategy literally the whole time… in the best way. Absolutely brilliant. So much thought and care was clearly put into this. Thank you so much for making this for us! 🩷💜🩵

Got this game as soon as it released,and it is awesome! The gameplay is fantastic and fair, there are even organized tournaments if log into there discord, player base is growing especially with Meta horizon. There are a few bugs but nothing too bad, highly recommend to those who love strategy games

Loved Moss 1 and 2 but was weary of a tabletop rts game but I was wrong! This has become one my favorite and addictive VR games. The matches are fun and action packed. I'm constantly thinking of new builds and figuring out what 3 champions will work best. The customization for the characters and unlocking new items has been fun. I love that there is only paid skins and not a pay to win system which makes it a lot more fair. Overall 5 stars with a very polished and great graphics.

Initially, I was uncertain whether Glassbreakers was the right path for the Moss universe, as it diverged significantly from the charming and appealing platform games that the series is known for. However, after spending some time with the game, my perspective has changed – the team has truly excelled with Glassbreakers. Polyarc has leveraged their expertise to create a completely original game for virtual reality, blending real-time strategy elements with the class-based systems typical of MOBA games, all while preserving the enchanting essence of the Moss universe. The game’s depth is remarkable; it’s much more than just achieving perfect synergy across a three-champion team – that’s merely the beginning of your strategy. Champions level up during matches by taking down opponents, and timing your attacks, managing power-ups, and capturing the sway stone can significantly shift the momentum and advantage in gameplay. Glassbreakers is competitive, incredibly addictive, and demands quick strategic adjustments and sharp thinking to maintain an edge in matches. The only notable drawback at the moment is that the player base hasn’t expanded sufficiently to ensure consistent matchmaking – however, there is an AI Skirmish mode available where you can progress and face AI opponents in these scenarios. One thing is certain: Glassbreakers offers something entirely unique in virtual reality, proving to be not only addictive but also one of the finest strategy experiences available in VR.

Makers of Moss have done it again. Glassbreakers is fantastically cinematic. There are similarities to Moss and why shouldn't it. I'm not sure why the Devs chose to go the table top mode when a free moving environment imho would've perhaps been a better option IMHO. Despite that it doesn't disappoint. I'm only a short way into the game so I haven't really been involved in the full experience. But these deva are probably as good as it gets. I purchased ahead of the release purely based on the Moss series. 
Initially I wrote that I was experiencing a couple of bugs. It was my left controller that kept disconnecting. I fixed that and all is working perfectly. My apologies for being a bit hasty if anyone had read it.

reviews.review_title: Great game! Horizon Plus must try!
reviews.review_description: It's a really fun tactical strategy game made like tower defense. Great gameplay, great cosmetics, growing player base. Matches aren't too long. Can easily do quick gaming sessions, or stay for hours. Really hope this game continues to grow and add content!
This game is in my top 3 VR experiences. I didn't even think I'd like tabletop style games but this one is just so dynamic and fun. Amazing art style. Quick matches.
This game is a pvp creature battler . Using characters from the moss series. It's basically capture the flag or in this case break the other players camp before they break yours.
Its just more of the same bit buggy in the game play how many moss games can you play? along with the clay shoot game 2 of the worst monthly titles ive had. But heyho still good value
This is a great moba that just so happens to be in vr. If you like tabletop mechanics you wouls appreciate it!

For those who remember a fantastic game called Brass Tactics from a few years back, this game really hits that same itch.

I love battling across a tabletop in VR. Nothing really compares — so many games are 1st person these days, and there aren’t enough games like this that really allow me to command troops. I love how the intimacy of Moss 1 and 2 is still somewhat intact here. 

Fantastic game. Trying to get my friends to pick it up as I write this.

In the beginning I didn’t like it felt chaotic and a free for all, then the game truly revealed itself to me. 

Glassbreakers is one of those games that you feel yourself getting better and figure out strategies from your experiences only to scrap those plans  mid game because of how it’s playing out. It’s a game you truly learn from your losses so much so that I wish it had a record option as to go back and study the outcome, so many times I thought victory was a glass stone throw away only to find myself on the losing end of things that I would love to see where it all went wrong.   Needless to say I love the game couldn’t put it down.  

The only negative I can say is and this could be because I’m early in progression, the majority of my matches are with AI. (I use intelligence loosely) ￼This of course is not the devs or games fault. Also with the character unlocks and all the cosmetics , this may be a me thing I was thinking it would just unlock characters and place them on the character wheel, but you actually have to go into the cosmetic portion of game and for each character as to find your unlocks. It was not explained. ￼Great Game Devs!

For those looking to play vs humans I recommend joining there discord great community and they line up games also a tournament is being scheduled for 12/13. See you there!

I mean - I love Moss, so maybe I’m biased but the gameplay for this is easy to learn and graphics are obviously gorgeous! 

I’ve only got to play with the 3 default champions so far. I’m wondering how I unlock the other champions though? 

I hope more people start playing this because I can’t be sure but it feels like I’m always playing AI at the moment?

reviews.review_title: The game is really good but
reviews.review_description: The game really has potential, not for me but it has potential

reviews.review_title: A fun and creative multiplayer game
reviews.review_description: So far I've played a few games solo, and a few with a friend in co-op, and Im really liking it. The controls are easy to get the hang of, and upgrading and unlocking characters is simple and straightforward. I also love the visuals. The art style of Moss has always been beautiful, and this game continues that tradition.
It is very nice. Familiar characters in a board game setting. Really enjoyable!
Lo

GlassBreakers returns as a premium title after two years of refinement, and the wait was worth it. This tactical RTS/MOBA drops you face-to-face with your opponent across a hex-based battlefield where adorable champions clash in intense 10-minute matches.

The genius lies in the team building. With 12 champions offering 220 possible three-character combinations, you're not just picking favorites, you're looking for synergies. Some excel at aggressive pushes, others at defensive strategies, and your entire playstyle shifts based on who you field. The tactical depth here genuinely surprised me.

Visually, it's pure Polyarc magic. The champion designs and animations are bursting with personality, and watching your cuddly warriors battle it out never gets old. 

The challenge? Player population is thin. You'll often face bots unless you coordinate matches through the active Discord community. It's not quite "jump in anytime" yet, but when you do find human opponents, the matches are thrilling. The momentum shifts are real! I've had games where crushing victory turned into devastating defeat in the final 30 seconds.

At $19.99, it's a polished, strategic gem that needs more players. If tactical VR battles in a charming fantasy world sound appealing, give it a shot and help grow the community.

For our full review, check the 6DOF Reviews website or our YouTube Channel!
W game
Couch game, yep hard to tack off. Good learning curve.
Such a fun game hard to put down honestly

Amazing game to play, super fun.
Perfectly built too, it plays so well for day one release which is something to be proud of on today's world.

I played the beta almost 2 years ago now, and have been looking forward to the full release since. The champions are very well-balanced, the boards all play differently, and the game has a lot of polish. I often times find myself picking up the models and just admiring the detail put into the game. Well worth the wait!!

My current favorite game! Knew right away from the preview that it was up my alley. Great competitive format where you aren't dependent on teammates. I really enjoy the fast pace and short matches. You also get to level up champions, rank up, and collect dyes/skins, which is great fun. Highly recommend!

I absolutely love this game. The basics are simple. Mastering the champions and the strategy is something you'll develop over time. My absolute favorite is the buddies mode where you have to coordinate your team with a friend.

reviews.review_title: Easy to learn, harder to master
reviews.review_description: This game accommodates everyone from beginner to master. Only want to play a quick game? You can jump in, select your champions and play a match in under 15 minutes. Want to curl up and play for hours? While each match is 9 minutes unless it goes to overtime, the unlocks happen at a steady and satisfying clip, and it is easier to get lost in Glassbreakers for hours than it is to stop at just one match. Either way, this game has your back. There are ranked matches for games against other glassbreakers, or you can play AI Skirmishes if you just want to work on your mastery track alone. Want to buddy up with a friend? You can. Playing is easy and engaging fun, but working out the strategies of different teams of champions will take more time. Ranked matches will never pit a new Bronze player against a Platinum player, but if a human can't be found for your ranked match, AI will step in and fill the gap, so new players will never be left behind and never held back no matter when they pick up the game and start breaking glass.

reviews.review_title: Very High Quality VR Title Yet Again!
reviews.review_description:
Polyarc has once again delivered another fantastic game to the VR platform. Its very cool seeing them take a shot at a different genre, and I think they nailed it straight off the bat.
The mechanics are intuitive, and are easy to get the hang of.
Visuals are absolutely stunning, they havent lost their glory since Moss Book II.
While there is no core story to this game, there's little lore tid bits for each character. And you can piece together bits of information you discover while sitting in the menu, which is really neat!
After being in the loop of its development for a two years, and seeing how far it's come, and Im super excited to see where the game goes from here!
Got it for free
Deserves a lot more attention. Great game,, thank you polyark

reviews.review_title: Dude, this game is absolutely INSANE!
reviews.review_description:
I was playing last night and literally couldn’t stop. Put together a squad with Barnard, Gwendoline, and Vendrix and just started crushing everyone in ranked. Matches are quick (like 8-10 mins), but every second is pure adrenaline – you literally see the other guy moving his pieces in real time and you’re like “holy cow, what’s he about to do???”
The VR immersion is on another level, feels like you’re actually leaning over the table battling it out. And best part: it’s 100% real free-to-play, zero pay-to-win nonsense. Played like 20 games and already unlocked 3 more champions just from XP, didn’t spend a dime.
Seriously, if you have a Quest or Vision Pro, download this RIGHT NOW. I’m completely hooked.
Easy 5 stars, best VR multiplayer I’ve played all year! 🏆

So far the game is fun there’s a bit of a learning curve, but nothing to overwhelming. My only complaint is what happened to the free DLC that you were supposed to get when you pre-ordered? I didn’t see any of it or I’m not looking in the right part of the menu but it would be nice to get that and the rating will probably go up to what I really wanna give it

developer_response.body: Jimbot5000 - Let's get this figured out. Connect with us at https://player.support.polyarcgames.com and we'll get this straightened out. - Lil L

reviews.review_description:
The game has beautiful graphics and the gameplay is simple but engaging.

However, there is no campaign to follow and it’s a tad misleading with the 2v2 mentioned. You and your friend will not get your own troops but will have to share and move the 3. It’s a little bit of a let down and something that was not clear.

Unlocking troops and skins is fun, you level up your little guys and get dyes and new champions as you level up each champion.

The devs have been super communicative in the discord and they’ve been fixing any issues that have arisen. I really appreciate this and hope they continue to improve the game!

developer_response.body:
Hey LunarHalcyon!

This is a great piece of feedback about Buds Mode. I've gone ahead and passed it onto the team. Thank you for taking the time to write a review!

reviews.review_description:
Hands down one of the best games available in VR, and definitely THE best real time strategy game.

Characters are all so wonderfully designed and animated, the controls are perfect for the genre - it really is a masterpiece.

The nuance and depth to the gameplay is fantastic, every match feels different. The team building possibilities are unbelievably rich, and trying out new teams and strategies is so much fun.

Honestly cannot recommend this game highly enough. One of my top 3 all time video games
good game but its just missing somthing
only problem where quill?
The gameplay is simple to pick up and play. However the more time you spend with it, the deeper the mechanics go. It's a great game and I highly recommend it.

reviews.review_title: Tons of battle fun with Moss characters
reviews.review_description: What a fun way to build a deeper relationship with all the beautiful Moss characters! I’m not typically a player of these games because I don’t have interest in the  characters, but after having played Moss before, I’ve been happily drawn into this gameplay style because of the wonderful characters. I’m truly enjoying leveling up their skills and wardrobes. Thanks Polyarc for such a wonderful addition to my Vr game collection!
it's a basically a tower fence kind of

reviews.review_title: A Gem that deserves more players
reviews.review_description:
I've played Glassbreakers long enough to unlock all 12 heroes, and the game's quality is obvious. The world is visually striking, polished, and packed with personality.

PvP matches are genuinely challenging, you win only if you read the field fast, sync abilities well, and build smart hero combos. Even with the current roster, there's plenty of room for creative strategies.

The game's universe adds real depth instead of being just decoration, and that keeps matches fresh. It's clear the devs know exactly what they're doing with this setting.

Honestly, the only thing missing is a bigger player base and more content. The game deserves both. I'm looking forward to new heroes, and I recommend it without hesitation.

reviews.review_title: Fun /Unique entry into the Moss universe
reviews.review_description:
Having played the first Moss I knew the developers at Polyarc could create a compelling and beautiful game, but having played Glassbreakers both in Alpha and now upon full release I can say this game is a great entry into the "Mossiverse" entirely on its own.

Very unique in its gameplay and approach, Glassbreakers is a blend of parts of a MOBA, tabletop game, and RTS (although it probably most resembles a MOBA). But don't let these subgenres intimidate you as somehow Glassbreakers still manages to be approachable and not feel too chaotic. The control scheme helps to make the game work well especially during the intense moments in a battle and rounds take only about 10 minutes to complete.

My openly criticisms would be sometimes the controls, specifically the special abilities, can feel difficult to activate correctly (although this could just be me) and it can be difficult to find a match at odd times of day (although the discord seems to be helpful with this). Thank you Devs for another great game and I look forward to continuing to hone my skills in the arena.
Fun game to play.

reviews.review_title: This game is a fresh air to meta quest
reviews.review_description: I recommend this game by far. Glassbreakers requires you to full focus on the match and be extremely strategic to win those games. They have a good roster with unique abilities and gameplay. The way you combine your heroes relies heavy in the style of game tou want to use. I will tryhard so much to support its competitive scene. 10/10

It had been a while since I last played games on VR, but this game got me back to playing daily. Glassbreakers is an RTS/MOBA game where you control 3 heroes on a board while your opponent also controls 3 heroes. It's not like conventional MOBAs where there are minions to tank the towers for you; you need to use your heroes for that. To assist in this task, there are areas in the game that you must control to gain items that increase your heroes' stats (such as health, damage, and ability damage), and a crystal that works as a buff. It weakens the opponent's towers and nexus for a while.
The game is visually stunning, the community is wonderful, and the developers are very caring, so I don't see any downsides to not trying out this game. Furthermore, the game is free, so you just need to download and play.
For those who complain about only facing bots, you just need to show up on Discord and look for someone to play with. The community is welcoming, just type over there.

reviews.review_title: Glassbreakers - A True Champion of Moss
reviews.review_description:
Right from the go, this is simply fun to play. 

Matches are fast, fluid, and tactical without being obtuse. Tutorials are strong and teach you the basic mechanics while also hinting at more advanced techs.  There's a great onboarding platform here. 

Once into the main loop of matches, review, repeat, you'll find a good number of unlockables including a strong range of playable characters and cosmetics. All unlocked through playing not paying. Win. 

The matches themselves are surprisingly strategic with you choosing where to focus your three characters' attentions, and choosing when to use special abilities. Like all the great games, it is simple to understand yet has depth and complexities you can't even begin to consider at first. 

Similar to Moss and Moss Book II, this is now a must buy on VR. That is especially true if you enjoy real time strategy games.

I love the world of Moss. I finished the two first games. I was expecting a lot of this one. Unfortunatly, the matchmaking is not working because there is not enough players to play with. So you finished playing with AI.

developer_response.body:
Hi Charlous!

Thanks for your review! While Glassbreakers grows, we know that there are windows of time when player populations are low, so you'll often queue into more AI matches. Sorry about that!

We've recently established some community play times inside our Discord, which is quite active with players coordinating play times, discussing comps, planning tournaments, and more. We encourage you to join one of these community play events and see how you feel! Thanks again! We really appreciate you taking the time to leave a review. :)

reviews.review_description:
As a fan of both Moss games I just had to pre-order this one and it’s exactly what’s promised. 

Make sure you use the countdown for a battle to take in the beautiful scenery around the battle area!
Very fun game that is strategy dependent and engaging. I love how much it expands on the world of Moss. Can’t wait to see more!￼

I wish the solo game had more of a framework other than just a simple skirmish mode, but versus games often live or die on multiplayer play, so that is expected.  The characters are fun and cute, with really well done animations.

reviews.review_title: Very Pretty and a Near Miss
reviews.review_description:
Glassbreakers is a VR competitive game that delivers intense, engaging gameplay right out of the gate. The action is visceral and keeps you on your toes, making for thrilling matches. However, the experience falls short of its considerable potential in several key areas.
The game forces you to start with three preset characters, with additional ones unlocking later. Character customisation exists, but it's purely functional. You're colouring them to distinguish them visually during battle rather than expressing yourself. This shallow approach to character building misses an opportunity to create genuine investment in your team.

More troubling is the monetisation strategy. At nineteen dollars for entry, the immediate barrage of microtransaction prompts for more dollars for skin sets feels exploitative. You've already paid for the game; asking for additional money to look presentable left a sour taste.

The lack of voice communication is a glaring omission. There's no smack talk, no camaraderie, no celebration—just silent competition. In a multiplayer game, that human element matters tremendously, and Glassbreakers removes it entirely.

Finally, the game lacks spectator features. No way to watch live battles or review past matches from the leaderboard. These tools would enrich the competitive experience and help players improve.

Glassbreakers has solid foundations, but it needs meaningful depth in mechanics, fairer monetisation, social features, and spectator tools to realise its promise. Right now, it's a decent diversion rather than a compelling experience.

reviews.review_title: Beautiful but crashy and no one online
reviews.review_description: Regret this christmas purchase. 😑

developer_response.body:
Hi Seal, I'm sorry to hear that you're experiencing crashes. We've recently deployed a patch that aimed to resolve some of the issues we were seeing that caused crashes and instability. Please feel free to jump back into the game and see if things are feeling better.

As for the second issue (low player population), while Glassbreakers grows, there are times of day where player populations are low. To remedy that, we've established some community play times inside our Discord, which is quite active with Glassbreakers players coordinating play, discussing comps, planning tournaments, and more. We encourage you to join one of these community play events and see how you feel!

Thanks for your review - we read every player review and take your feedback seriously.

reviews.review_title: A brilliant expansion of Moss’s world
reviews.review_description:
When I finished the second Moss game years ago, I told anyone who would listen to me that the world of Moss could easily be VR’s Mario. A world & characters that can work in multiple genres, not just adventure games. ￼￼

Glassbreakers is proof of that. Everything here feels familiar to anyone who has played the previous games, just presented in a new way with really fun, new characters and an addicting gameplay loop. ￼

VR has precious few MOBA adjacent games and I think that’s nuts given how big the genre fanbase is, so I’m glad Polyarc has taken up the challenge. 

Excellent game.
I love the Moss games and this is a great spin-off!!

reviews.review_title: Best VR Moba And Strategy Game
reviews.review_description: Best game I've ever played, addictive and challenging. Always testing strategies and improving mechanics. The learning curve is big so there's no way to get sick. I highly recommend it!
Top spiel
Great game. But I wish you could turn off bots, queue with higher level players and maybe had more MR features.

reviews.review_description: Its been a long time since I’ve been as motivated or engrossed by a VR game - 5/5, I highly recommend to anyone who’s looking for a fun and engaging VR experience with rewarding PvP, PvE, and progression.

It's an RTS in the style of Moss so what's not to love.

Interesting characters to unlock and build a team with for rapid RTS/MOBA games.

Tons of content and a beautiful world what more could you want.
a solid game and the great addition to the other moss games looking forward to further expansions to this
It's been a while since I played a fun and polished VR title like this one. It's really good.
this game's really different from moss one and two but it still felt really good to be back in this world and this game is really fun.

reviews.review_description: The level of care put in this game is incredible. The characters feel alive and the action on the board is frantic. Can’t recommend it enough.

reviews.review_description: Great game. Graphics are amazing with gameplay to match. Well done team.
Nothing like the others games  . Seem to be in the same place all the time just fighting

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review patpete61! Glassbreakers is designed as a tactical arena-strategy game, so it may differ from what you expected. We’re grateful you gave it a shot. If you’re open to it, we’d love to hear how we can improve clarity: https://player.support.polyarcgames.com

reviews.review_description:
I dropped in and played! As a long time fan of Moss (and the music) I wasn't quite sure what to expect. 

Like some of the other reviews, this definitely gives great tabletop and RTS vibes. It also reminds me of brass tactics. Its great to see these type of games being made in VR. 

The music, character design and more are on point. The tutorial was easy to understand. Really pretty looking strategy game, maybe even the best in VR.

The only thing I would say is that joining the discord is probably the best as it is a fairly complex game once you end up playing against someone else and lots of good tips in there.
Thank you so much
Great game. I love it and the challenge!!
Its ight

I am a big fan of Moss 1 and 2 so had to buy this.
I did the first tutorial game today with AI.  It’s certainly a learning curve but I think after a few games, I will find it a bit easier as at the moment I’m pressing all the buttons.  The graphics are amazing and I can see me playing this game a lot.
